DocJacket runs automated workflows behind the scenes to handle routine operations — like processing incoming emails, running compliance checks, sending reminders, and extracting data from documents.

You don't create or configure workflows yourself. They run automatically based on events in your transactions. But you can **monitor** them and **approve or reject** steps that need your input.

## Monitoring workflows

Go to **Settings > Workflows** to see:

- **Running workflows** — What's currently being processed
- **Completed workflows** — What finished successfully
- **Failed workflows** — Anything that hit an error (with retry options)
- **Awaiting approval** — Steps that need your manual approval before continuing
## Approval steps

Some workflows pause and wait for your input. For example, AI might extract data from a document and ask you to approve the results before saving them. When this happens, you'll see the workflow in "Awaiting Approval" status.

From the workflow detail view, you can:

- **Approve** — Let the workflow continue with the suggested action
- **Reject** — Stop the workflow from proceeding
- **Retry** — Re-run a failed step
- **Cancel** — Stop the workflow entirely

## What workflows handle

Behind the scenes, workflows power features like:

- Document processing and compliance scanning
- Email intake and routing
- AI data extraction
- Reminder notifications
- Morning briefings
- Task and milestone completion actions


# Workflows

DocJacket runs automated workflows behind the scenes to handle routine operations — like processing incoming emails, running compliance checks, sending reminders, and extracting data from documents.

You don't create or configure workflows yourself. They run automatically based on events in your transactions. But you can **monitor** them and **approve or reject** steps that need your input.

## Monitoring workflows

Go to **Settings > Workflows** to see:

- **Running workflows** — What's currently being processed
- **Completed workflows** — What finished successfully
- **Failed workflows** — Anything that hit an error (with retry options)
- **Awaiting approval** — Steps that need your manual approval before continuing
## Approval steps

Some workflows pause and wait for your input. For example, AI might extract data from a document and ask you to approve the results before saving them. When this happens, you'll see the workflow in "Awaiting Approval" status.

From the workflow detail view, you can:

- **Approve** — Let the workflow continue with the suggested action
- **Reject** — Stop the workflow from proceeding
- **Retry** — Re-run a failed step
- **Cancel** — Stop the workflow entirely

## What workflows handle

Behind the scenes, workflows power features like:

- Document processing and compliance scanning
- Email intake and routing
- AI data extraction
- Reminder notifications
- Morning briefings
- Task and milestone completion actions

---

## Integrations


# Integrations

DocJacket connects with the tools you already use. Set up integrations in **Settings > Integrations**.

## Available integrations

### Google Workspace
- **Gmail** — Sync emails and attachments with transactions
- **Google Calendar** — Push closings, milestones, and tasks to your calendar
- **Google Drive** — Store and organize transaction documents
- **Google Contacts** — Import and sync your contacts

### Microsoft 365
- **Outlook Mail** — Sync emails with transactions
- **Outlook Calendar** — Sync calendar events and deadlines
- **OneDrive** — Cloud document storage and sync

### Webhooks
You can also set up custom webhooks in **Settings > Connections** to send event notifications to external services like Zapier, Slack, or Microsoft Teams.

## In this section

- [Google Workspace](./google-workspace.md) — Connect Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and Contacts
- [Microsoft Outlook](./microsoft-outlook.md) — Connect Outlook Mail, Calendar, and OneDrive
- [Webhooks](./webhooks.md) — Send events to external services

---

# Google Workspace

Connect your Google account to sync email, documents, and calendar events with DocJacket.

## What's included

### Gmail
- Send emails from DocJacket using your Gmail account
- Incoming emails are tracked and linked to transactions
- Email history syncs both ways

### Google Drive
- Import documents from Drive into any transaction
- Export documents from DocJacket back to Drive
- Browse your Drive folders directly from the Documents tab

### Google Calendar
- Sync transaction milestones and deadlines to your Google Calendar
- See DocJacket events alongside your personal calendar
- Two-way sync — changes in either place are reflected in both

## Connecting

1. Go to **Settings > Integrations**
2. Find **Google Workspace** and click **Connect**
3. Sign in with your Google account
4. Grant the requested permissions
5. You're connected
## Tips

- Use a dedicated Google account for your TC business if you want to keep personal and work email separate
- Google Drive imports are copies — changes in Drive won't sync to DocJacket automatically
- Calendar sync is especially useful if you manage your schedule through Google Calendar


# Integrations

DocJacket connects with the tools you already use. Set up integrations in **Settings > Integrations**.

## Available integrations

### Google Workspace
- **Gmail** — Sync emails and attachments with transactions
- **Google Calendar** — Push closings, milestones, and tasks to your calendar
- **Google Drive** — Store and organize transaction documents
- **Google Contacts** — Import and sync your contacts

### Microsoft 365
- **Outlook Mail** — Sync emails with transactions
- **Outlook Calendar** — Sync calendar events and deadlines
- **OneDrive** — Cloud document storage and sync

### Webhooks
You can also set up custom webhooks in **Settings > Connections** to send event notifications to external services like Zapier, Slack, or Microsoft Teams.

## In this section

- [Google Workspace](./google-workspace.md) — Connect Gmail, Drive, Calendar, and Contacts
- [Microsoft Outlook](./microsoft-outlook.md) — Connect Outlook Mail, Calendar, and OneDrive
- [Webhooks](./webhooks.md) — Send events to external services

---

# Microsoft Outlook

Connect your Microsoft 365 account to sync email, calendar, and documents with DocJacket.

## What's included

### Outlook Mail
- Send emails from DocJacket using your Outlook account
- Incoming emails are tracked and linked to transactions
- Email history syncs both ways

### Outlook Calendar
- Sync transaction milestones and deadlines to your Outlook Calendar
- See DocJacket events alongside your existing schedule

### OneDrive
- Store and sync transaction documents with OneDrive
- Access your cloud files directly from DocJacket

## Connecting

1. Go to **Settings > Integrations**
2. Find **Microsoft 365** and click **Connect**
3. Sign in with your Microsoft account
4. Grant the requested permissions
5. You're connected
## Tips

- Works with Microsoft 365 business and personal accounts
- Calendar sync is two-way — changes in either place are reflected in both
- If your organization uses Microsoft 365 for email, this integration keeps everything in sync without switching between apps

---

# Webhooks

Webhooks let you send real-time event notifications from DocJacket to external services — like Zapier, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or your own custom applications.

## Setting up a webhook

1. Go to **Settings > Connections**
2. Click **Create Webhook**
3. Enter the **URL** where you want events sent
4. Choose which **event types** to subscribe to:
   - Document changes (uploaded, approved, rejected)
   - Transaction changes (created, updated, status changed)
   - Task changes (created, completed)
   - Milestone changes (completed, approaching)
5. Save the webhook

DocJacket will send a POST request to your URL every time a subscribed event occurs.
## Monitoring

The Connections page shows delivery stats for each webhook:

- **Success rate** — How many deliveries succeeded
- **Recent deliveries** — Status of the last several attempts
- **Failures** — If deliveries are failing, you'll see error details

DocJacket includes circuit breaker protection — if a webhook fails repeatedly, it's automatically paused to prevent flooding a down endpoint. You can re-enable it once the issue is resolved.

## Common uses

- **Slack notifications** — Get a message in Slack when a document is uploaded or a task is completed
- **Zapier automations** — Connect DocJacket events to thousands of other apps through Zapier
- **Custom dashboards** — Feed transaction events into your own reporting tools

---

## Reports


# Reports

DocJacket helps you generate professional reports and visualize your transaction pipeline.

## In this section

- [Transaction Reports](./transaction-reports.md) — Generate and share status reports
- [Pipeline View](./pipeline-view.md) — Visual Kanban board of your deals


# Reports

DocJacket helps you generate professional reports and visualize your transaction pipeline.

## In this section

- [Transaction Reports](./transaction-reports.md) — Generate and share status reports
- [Pipeline View](./pipeline-view.md) — Visual Kanban board of your deals

---

# Pipeline View

The Pipeline page gives you a Kanban-style board of all your transactions, organized by status. It's a visual way to see your workload at a glance.
## How it works

Each column represents a transaction status (Inbox, Active, Pending, Closed, etc.). Transaction cards live in the column matching their current status.

### Transaction cards

Each card shows:

- **Property address** — Click to jump into the transaction
- **Transaction type** — Purchase, sale, lease, etc.
- **Purchase price** — The deal value
- **Closing date** — When it's expected to close

### Drag and drop

Move cards between columns to update their status instantly. Drag a transaction from "Active" to "Closed" and the status updates in the background.

### Inbox column

New intakes land in the Inbox column. Click a card to review it and convert it into a full transaction.

## When to use Pipeline vs. List

- **Pipeline** is great for a visual overview of where all your deals stand — especially helpful in team meetings or for quick status checks
- **List view** (the Transactions page) is better for searching, filtering, and working with lots of transactions at once

Both views show the same data — just different ways of looking at it.

---

# Transaction Reports

Generate professional status reports to share with clients, agents, or your team. Reports pull live data from the transaction and can be exported as PDFs.

## Generating a report

1. Open a transaction
2. Go to the **Reports** section
3. See a live preview of the report
## Customizing sections

Toggle which sections to include in the report:

- **Milestones** — Timeline progress and upcoming deadlines
- **Tasks** — Task completion status
- **Documents** — Document checklist status
- **Contacts** — Key parties involved
- **Financials** — Purchase price, earnest money, loan details

The preview updates in real time as you toggle sections on and off, so you can see exactly what the final report will look like before generating the PDF.

## Sharing reports

Once you're happy with the report, you can:

- **Download as PDF** — Save it locally or attach it to an email
- **Send directly** — Email the report to contacts from within DocJacket

---

## Settings & Account


# Settings & Account

Configure your organization, manage team members, set up integrations, and customize how DocJacket works for you.

Access Settings from the sidebar — it's at the bottom of the navigation.

## In this section

- [Organization Settings](./organization-settings.md) — Branding, defaults, and company info
- [User Management](./user-management.md) — Add and manage team members
- [Notification Preferences](./notification-preferences.md) — Control what alerts you receive


# Settings & Account

Configure your organization, manage team members, set up integrations, and customize how DocJacket works for you.

Access Settings from the sidebar — it's at the bottom of the navigation.

## In this section

- [Organization Settings](./organization-settings.md) — Branding, defaults, and company info
- [User Management](./user-management.md) — Add and manage team members
- [Notification Preferences](./notification-preferences.md) — Control what alerts you receive

---

# Notification Preferences

Control what notifications you receive and how you receive them.

## Configuring notifications

Go to **Settings > Notifications** to manage your preferences. You can toggle notifications for different event types:

- **Task reminders** — Get notified when tasks are approaching or overdue
- **Milestone alerts** — Notifications for upcoming or passed deadlines
- **Document updates** — When documents are uploaded, approved, or need review
- **Email activity** — When new emails arrive for your transactions
- **Workflow approvals** — When a workflow step needs your approval

## Daily digest

DocJacket can send you a daily morning briefing email that summarizes:

- Closings happening today
- Tasks due today
- Overdue items
- Pending documents

Toggle this on from your Dashboard or in notification settings.

## Tips

- Start with all notifications on, then turn off the ones that aren't useful for your workflow
- The morning briefing is a great way to start your day with a clear picture of what needs attention
- Notification settings are per-user — each team member controls their own preferences

---

# Organization Settings

Customize DocJacket to match your brand and workflow preferences.

## Company information

Set your organization's basic info:

- **Company name** — Appears in emails, reports, and the client portal
- **Address and phone** — Contact details for your organization
- **Logo** — Uploaded logo appears on the portal, reports, and outgoing communications
## Branding

Your brand identity carries through DocJacket:

- **Logo** — Used in the client portal, email headers, and reports
- **Colors** — Customize accent colors for the portal

## Default settings

Configure defaults that apply to new transactions:

- **Default transaction type** — Pre-select the most common type
- **Default timeline template** — Auto-apply a template to new transactions
- **Custom fields** — Add custom fields to capture data specific to your workflow

## Transaction roles

In **Settings > Transaction Roles**, you can create and manage the contact roles available when adding contacts to a transaction. The built-in roles (Buyer, Seller, Agent, etc.) cover most needs, but you can add custom ones if your workflow requires it.

---

# User Management

Add team members to your DocJacket organization and control what they can access.

## Inviting team members

1. Go to **Settings > Users**
2. Click **Invite User**
3. Enter their email address
4. Assign a role (which controls their permissions)
5. Send the invitation

They'll receive an email with a link to join your organization.

## Roles and permissions

Each team member is assigned a role that controls what they can see and do. You can create custom roles in **Settings > Roles** with granular permissions for:

- **Organization management** — Access to settings and organization config
- **User management** — Ability to invite and manage other users
- **Transaction management** — Create, edit, delete transactions
- **Communication** — Send emails, access inbox
- **Tasks & scheduling** — Manage tasks and calendar
- **Reports & analytics** — View and generate reports
- **Billing** — Access subscription and payment details
- **API & integrations** — Manage integrations and webhooks
## Managing users

From the Users page in Settings, you can:

- **Change a user's role** — Update their permissions
- **Deactivate a user** — Remove their access without deleting their account
- **Remove a user** — Permanently remove them from your organization

---

## Billing & Plans


# Billing & Plans

Manage your DocJacket subscription, view your plan details, and handle payment information.

## In this section

- [Plans & Pricing](./plans-and-pricing.md) — What's included in each plan
- [Managing Your Subscription](./managing-your-subscription.md) — Upgrade, downgrade, or update payment info


# Billing & Plans

Manage your DocJacket subscription, view your plan details, and handle payment information.

## In this section

- [Plans & Pricing](./plans-and-pricing.md) — What's included in each plan
- [Managing Your Subscription](./managing-your-subscription.md) — Upgrade, downgrade, or update payment info

---

# Managing Your Subscription

Handle all billing and subscription tasks from **Settings > Billing** (or the **Billing** link in the sidebar).

## Viewing your plan

The billing page shows:

- Your current plan and pricing
- Number of seats (active users)
- Next billing date
- Payment method on file

## Adding or removing seats

As your team grows or shrinks:

- **Add a seat** — Invite a new team member and a seat is added automatically. You'll be billed for the additional seat on your next billing cycle.
- **Remove a seat** — Deactivate a user in Settings > Users. The seat is freed up and your bill adjusts on the next cycle.

## Updating payment info

Click **Update Payment Method** on the billing page to change your credit card or payment details.

## Canceling

If you need to cancel your subscription:

1. Go to **Settings > Billing**
2. Click **Cancel Subscription**
3. Your account remains active until the end of the current billing period

Your data is retained for a period after cancellation in case you want to come back.

## Questions?

For billing questions or issues, reach out to [hello@docjacket.com](mailto:hello@docjacket.com). We're happy to help.

---

# Plans & Pricing

DocJacket offers simple per-seat pricing. Every user on your team gets a seat, and you can add or remove seats as your team changes.

## What's included

All paid plans include the full DocJacket feature set:

- Unlimited transactions
- AI contract extraction and task generation
- Timeline and task templates
- Document management and DocDrop
- Client portal
- Email integration (Gmail and Outlook)
- Calendar sync
- Reports and pipeline view
- Team management and roles

## Free plan

DocJacket offers a free plan with limited active transactions — great for trying out the platform before committing.

## Current pricing

Visit [docjacket.com/pricing](https://docjacket.com/pricing) for the latest pricing details and plan options.

## Early-bird pricing

If you signed up during the early-bird period, your locked-in rate stays the same for as long as you maintain your subscription. This rate is grandfathered and won't increase.
